scenario: Aurora Outdoors sends an order confirmation summary to 4500 customers per month through a Camunda 8.8 templated email task that uses Feelers. The data carries a top-level currency field and an items array, where each item has a name and a price property, and the template must render one line per item showing the item name and the currency-prefixed price.
question: Which Feelers template correctly renders the per-item lines using the documented loop and accessor rules?

options.text: A template with a loop over items that references the item name as this.name and the price as parent.currency joined to price on each rendered line
A template with a loop over items that references the item name as name and the outer currency as parent.currency joined to price on each rendered line
A template with an if block over items that references the name and the currency through the parent accessor on every iteration of the items array
A template with a loop over items that references the currency as this.currency and the name as parent.name on each rendered line of the output

a.text: Incorrect. Inside a loop the object properties are referenced directly by their key, so using this.name introduces an unnecessary accessor that does not match the documented pattern for object items.

b.text: Correct. Inside a loop, object properties are referenced by their key directly, and data outside the item scope, such as the top-level currency, is reached through the parent accessor.

c.text: Incorrect. The if tag conditionally renders a block, but iteration over an array requires the loop tag, so the per-item lines would not repeat with an if construct.

d.text: Incorrect. Within the loop, currency lives in the outer scope and must be reached through parent, while name belongs to the current item and is referenced directly.

explanation: Feelers loops expose object properties of the current item by their key. To reach data outside the item scope, such as the top-level currency, the template uses the parent accessor, so name resolves to the item property and parent.currency reaches the outer field.
